task_completion.description: Verify that an IFC4 project has been created or initialized.
task_completion.visual_evidence: BlenderBIM interface showing an active IFC4 project setup in the Scene properties or the Outliner panel.

task_completion.description: Verify that at least one column and one beam are modeled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: 3D viewport showing a column and a beam intersecting or joining, with the Outliner confirming elements classified as IfcColumn and IfcBeam.

task_completion.description: Verify that a flat steel plate is modeled at the connection joint.
task_completion.visual_evidence: 3D viewport showing a flat plate located at the joint between the column and beam, with the Outliner or Object properties confirming it is classified as IfcPlate.

task_completion.description: Verify that a bolt pattern of at least four individual bolts is modeled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: 3D viewport showing a pattern of at least four individual bolts at the connection joint, with the Outliner confirming they are classified as IfcFastener.

task_completion.description: Verify that a material with 'Steel' in its name is assigned to the connection elements.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Material properties panel showing a material containing 'Steel' in its name assigned to the plate and/or fasteners.

task_completion.description: Verify that the completed model is saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File browser, save dialog, or terminal showing the file 'steel_connection.ifc' being saved in the '/home/ga/BIMProjects/' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Blender GUI to model the connection.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with Blender's 3D viewport, Outliner, and properties panels rather than just terminal commands echoing text into a file.

integrity.description: Verify that genuine 3D geometry was created for the structural elements.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D viewport displays solid 3D meshes for the column, beam, plate, and bolts, not just empty objects or 2D sketches.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actively modeled the components rather than copying a pre-existing file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the step-by-step progression of adding and positioning the beam, column, plate, and bolts in the Blender session.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified facts: The expected output path is exactly '/home/ga/BIMProjects/steel_connection.ifc'. The material assigned to the connection elements must contain the keyword 'steel' (case-insensitive). There must be a minimum of 4 fasteners (IfcFastener) in the model. The task requires creating a new IFC4 model from an empty Blender session.
